page: Microsoft Azure FX48-12mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmdsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 6 physical cores and 12 virtual CPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes 48 KB of L1D cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 260 MB of L3 cache, with support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AMX. The server is equipped with 1008.0 GB of system memory, yielding an exceptionally high resource density of 84.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Additionally, it includes 2835 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the FX48-12mds_v2 demonstrates strong single-core capabilities. It achieves a top-tier Geekbench single-core score of 2365.0 and a text processing score of 2927.0, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks are also highly efficient, with gzip compression reaching 34.55 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression reaching 14.47 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 25%. Multi-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance at 13318.5 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11420.0, both falling within the middle 50% average. Memory bandwidth is measured at 1033.73 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 102.55 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

The hardware profile of this server represents a specific tradeoff, prioritizing massive memory capacity and high single-core clock speeds over high core counts. This makes the server qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that are memory-intensive but do not scale linearly across massive parallel core arrays. The presence of 2835 GB of local storage combined with 1008.0 GB of RAM makes it suitable for large in-memory databases, memory-heavy enterprise applications, and data-intensive processing tasks. However, for highly parallelized multi-threaded workloads, the 12 vCPU limit may act as a performance bottleneck compared to servers with higher core densities.
description: Microsoft Azure FX48-12mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server from the FXmdsv2 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 6 physical cores and 12 vCPUs running at 4.2 GHz. It features an exceptionally high memory capacity of 1008.0 GB, providing 84.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside 2835 GB of local storage. In benchmark testing, the server excels in single-core operations, achieving top-tier results in Geekbench single-core and text processing tests, alongside strong single-threaded compression performance. Multi-core benchmarks and memory bandwidth fall into the average range, reflecting a design that prioritizes massive memory capacity and high single-thread clock speeds over massive parallel core counts. This configuration is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, in-memory databases, and enterprise applications requiring substantial RAM-to-vCPU ratios without the need for extensive multi-threaded scaling.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX48-12mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 12 vCPUs, 1008 GB of RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX48-12mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 12 vCPUs, 1008 GB of RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-intensive server combining a high-frequency Intel processor, massive memory capacity, and local storage for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
